figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Maximum eigenvalues of Q/c for the semi-implicit scheme. (b) Maximum eigenvalues of Q/c for the scheme (3.5)–(3.7). (c) Number of Newton’s iteration for δt = 1.
Figure 2: (a) Energy. (b) The concentration and eigenvalue at t = 50, computed on a mesh N = 64 and time step δt = 3.125× 10−4. The principal eigenvectors are plotted in the figure of concentration.
Figure 3: The error ε with different (a) time steps for N = 64; and (b) spatial discretizations with δt = h2/10.
Figure 4: Snapshots of the solution for c0 = 20 and L = √ 20. The colorbar is the same as the density profile shown in Fig.2.
Figure 5: The steady states for (a) c0 = 5, L = √ 10; (b) c0 = 10, L = √ 10; (c) c0 = 20, L = √ 5; (d) c0 = 20, L = √ 20.
Figure 6: The steady states for L = 4d correspond to the initial states of (a) uniform vertical alignment and (b) uniform 135◦ alignment.

Figure 7: When the initial state consists of left-half 45◦ alignment and right-half 135◦ alignment for L = 8d, the total energy versus time is shown in (a), and (b)-(f) are snapshots of the solution evolution at t = 20, 40, 70, 120, 160. The color bar corresponds to the density profile shown in Fig.6.

Figure 8: When the initial state is set to be 135◦ alignment for L = 8d, the total energy versus time is shown in (a), and (b)-(f) are snapshots of the solution evolution at t = 10, 30, 60, 100, 150. The colorbar follows the same scale as the density profile shown in Fig.6.
